methods.description: Process for measuring skeletal elements to gather ecomorphological data, focusing on elements present in the Bovid Hill Rusingoryx sample.

steps.description: Focus on elements or portions of elements present in the Bovid Hill Rusingoryx sample.
steps.searchable_summary: select skeletal elements from Bovid Hill Rusingoryx sample

steps.description: Measure the length and diameters of the distal and proximal ends, and dimensions of the articular surfaces using digital Mitutoyo calipers or a Paleo-Tech osteometric board.
steps.searchable_summary: measure skeletal elements using calipers and osteometric board

methods.description: Statistical method to predict habitats of fossil individuals by comparing them with extant known-habitat individuals using multivariate predictive statistics.

steps.description: Assign each individual in the comparative sample to an ecological category based on known species behavior.
steps.searchable_summary: assign ecological categories to comparative sample

steps.description: Use measurements of each element as predictor variables to determine linear dimensions for habitat group discrimination.
steps.searchable_summary: use element measurements as predictor variables in DFA

steps.description: Calculate probability for group membership based on proximity to centroids and evaluate model success by correct assignment rates.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate group membership probability and evaluate DFA model success
